informal: Prove that a group of order 56 has a normal Sylow $p$-subgroup for some prime $p$ dividing its order.
lean:
theorem Dummit_Foote_exercise_4_5_13 {G : Type*} [Group G] [Fintype G]
  (hG : card G = 56) :
  ∃ (p : ℕ) (P : Sylow p G), p.Prime ∧ (p ∣ card G) ∧ P.Normal
reasoning: The Lean statement asserts that for a finite group G of order 56, there exists a prime p dividing |G| and a Sylow p-subgroup P of G such that P is normal. This matches the informal claim exactly: existence of a prime p dividing the order with a normal Sylow p-subgroup. The Fintype assumption is needed in Lean to make sense of `card G = 56`, which corresponds to the informal "group of order 56". No extra hypotheses or weaker conclusions are introduced.
